title: Saint Francis of Assisi-  A Biography
author: Johannes Jorgensen
description: Born into the wealth of a prosperous cloth merchant in Assisi, Francis (1182-1226) initially lived the carefree life typical of his status, even serving as a soldier. However, in 1205, a transformative vision during a war led him back to Assisi, where he renounced his worldly pursuits to embrace a life of evangelical poverty, following the example of Jesus Christ. His journey took him to Rome, where he humbly begged for alms alongside the destitute at St. Peters Basilica. Upon returning to Assisi, he began preaching in the town square, quickly gathering a diverse following that grew into a global religious order. The Franciscans ignited a spiritual revival that illuminated the Dark Ages, a legacy that resonates even today. Johannes Jorgensen (1866‚Äì1956), renowned outside Denmark for his cherished biography of St. Francis, penned this work in 1907. His own spiritual journey‚Äîmarked by a quest for truth through various philosophies‚Äîwas profoundly impacted by a visit to Assisi in 1894, which ignited a deep love for Francis and ultimately led to his conversion to Catholicism in 1896. Jorgensens timeless biography continues to inspire readers across all backgrounds.

author: Stan Watkins, Bible Teacher
description:
The Bible was written long ago, in a foreign language, within an alien culture, using unfamiliar literary styles. This verse-by-verse Bible study confronts those challenges to discover God’s wisdom for life today.


Your teacher, Stan Watkins, has always founded his personal faith on the Bible, leading him to complete a Bible degree and enter Christian ministry, ultimately going with his wife and family to Europe for 6 years. There he served in the areas of Bible teaching and music. Since returning to the states, he has supported his local church as he has had opportunity, but now he would like a more regular outlet for teaching God’s word. He has been married to his wife, Mary, since 1990 and has three adult children. In addition to this podcast, he maintains an active piano teaching studio.

Catch On Fire Podcasts — Igniting Hearts With Biblical Truth, Historical Insight & Christian Discipleship

Welcome to Catch On Fire Podcasts, a Christian podcast dedicated to helping believers of all stages grow in understanding, boldness, and spiritual maturity through the study of God’s Word. In a world filled with noise, confusion, and spiritual distraction, this channel exists to bring clarity by returning to the unchanging truth of Scripture. Here, you will find teachings grounded in the Bible, supported by historical context, and connected to practical, current-day examples that help make the Word of God both understandable and actionable in everyday life.

My goal is simple: to help you catch on fire for Jesus Christ by deepening your relationship with Scripture and strengthening your walk as a disciple. Whether you're new to the faith, rediscovering your passion for God, or seeking to grow as a committed follower of Christ, this channel provides biblical teaching designed to transform your heart, renew your mind, and inspire you to live out your purpose.

✨ What This Channel Is All About

title: The MacArthur Center Podcast
author: The Master's Seminary
description: As The MacArthur Center for Expository Preaching at The Master’s Seminary opens its doors, season one of its flagship podcast will tell the surprising, providential, compelling story of how John MacArthur became synonymous with expository preaching. From his first sermon at Grace Community Church on February 9, 1969, through his 52 years of relentless, verse-by-verse exposition from the New Testament, John has modeled a simple idea: that the Bible is clear and the preacher’s task is to patiently explain that meaning to his audience. This documentary-style season will look at Grace Community Church, which during John’s 52-year ministry has grown from a small, nondescript church in the middle of the San Fernando Valley into one of the largest, most influential churches in the United States. These eight episodes will tell the story of John’s calling into pastoral ministry and explain why, and how, he preaches the way he does. It will also trace his involvement in controversies and look at how he preaches during cultural upheaval and personal suffering. Don’t miss season one of The MacArthur Center podcast.

title: Imagine Heaven Podcast with John Burke
author: John Burke
description:
From the author of the New York Times Bestseller Imagine Heaven—with over 1 million copies sold—comes a thought-provoking podcast by John Burke exploring one of life’s biggest questions: What happens after we die? 

The Gallop poll found that 1 in 25 Americans has experienced a Near-Death Experience (NDE). Join us as we unpack real-life NDE stories and examine how they align with the Heaven described in the Bible. 

This podcast dives into the intersection of faith, science, and the profound mystery of the afterlife. 

Prepare to be inspired, challenged, and awakened to new possibilities.

Tune in and discover: Are NDEs glimpses of Heaven?

Lloyd-Jones challenges preachers to take their calling seriously, provides practical direction for preparing sermons, sharing insights on the shape and form of a message as well as covering such topics as the use of humor, giving invitations in a message and the preacher’s relationship to the congregation.

As Christians, we often feel alone in the hard places—facing deep pain, doubt, shame, betrayal, and unfinished seasons. But you’re not alone.

I’m the friend who won’t leave you stuck in the tough middle space. Each week, we dive into faith that holds up in real life. No surface-level talk. No filters. No easy answers.

Hard questions. Honest hope. Jesus in it all. Come with me as we face it head-on and discover the place where raw faith and deep pain meet a relentless God.


Follow me @drgladyschilds on Instagram and visit gladyschilds.com for more.

title: Preaching to Myself
author: Jon Hill
description:
A podcast about figuring out faith.

Hosted by Jon Hill, Preaching to Myself is about sharing in some of the struggles, victories, and questions we have about following Jesus. No more fake faith, this is about being real, open, and honest with who we are and where God wants us to be.

I'm just preaching to myself, but I hope it's beneficial for you, too!

Join in and lets learn and grow in our faith together.

title: Keeping Families Connected
author: Keeping Families Connected
description:
Welcome to the Keeping Families Connected podcast!

We’re a Christ-centered ministry that helps families struggling with challenges that are dividing them. Whether you realize it or not, you and your family are targets in a spiritual and cultural battle. 

We’re here to equip you for that battle, strengthen your walk with the Lord, and to bring you scriptural truth, encouragement, healing and hope. 

We address issues like faith, marriage, divorce and how to protect your children. We help you identify spiritual and cultural threats and share biblical principles to overcome them.  

Would you like to gain valuable insight into yourself and those around you? Learn how to be a better person, spouse or parent? Are you willing to honestly assess your faith and then make changes to grow deeper in your relationship with God?

If you’re serious about your faith this is the place to be.  

My name is Rick Nischalke. My wife Barbara and I are co-founders of this ministry. 

You can find out more about us at Keepingfamiliesconnected.com.

If you’re a deeply committed Christian, you’ll love this podcast. If you’re not, prepare to discover a life filled with peace, strength and purpose. And to

title: Martyn Iles - At the Crux
author: Martyn Iles
description: The Revelation of Jesus Christ is a vision of history from heaven's perspective. As an x-ray unveils hidden realities of the body, Revelation unveils hidden realities of history - not merely as it appears to our senses, but as it truly is, in its fulness. The key is Jesus Christ. Having conquered this world's system, He now rules it. He is conducting history to God's perfect destiny. What is the secret to the power behind His rule? It is His cross. The Revelation of Jesus Christ calls this generation to live lives that rhyme with His, lives that conquer the world in the power of the cross.

title: Sermons by Pastor Jim Brown: A Christian Podcast
author: Pastor Jim Brown
description: Welcome to Sermons by Pastor Jim Brown: A Christian Podcast! Join Pastor Jim for uplifting Christian messages that blend humor and heartfelt wisdom. If you're looking for Christian podcasts, dive into powerful sermons on faith and hope, packed with biblical insights that inspire and equip you to trust God in hard times. Explore the power of faith and prayer while seeking Bible teaching, tips on how to grow in faith, or simply a dose of encouragement. Tune in to these Christian sermons online that strengthen your walk with God and deepen your understanding of Christian discipleship!

title: SERMONS ON THE GOSPEL OF MATTHEW (I) - WHEN CAN A CHRISTIAN HAVE AN INTIMATE CONVERSATION WITH THE LORD?
author: The New Life Mission
description: The Apostle Matthew is telling us that the Word of Jesus was spoken to everyone in this world, for he saw Jesus as the King of kings. Now, Christians throughout the world, who have just been born again by believing in the gospel of the water and the Spirit that we are spreading, are indeed yearning to feed on the bread of life. But it is difficult for them to have fellowship with us in the true gospel, for they are all far away from us. Therefore, to meet the spiritual needs of these people of Jesus Christ, the King of kings, the sermons in this book have been prepared as new bread of life for them to nourish their spiritual growth. The author proclaims that those who have received the remission of their sins by believing in the Word of Jesus Christ, the King of kings, must feed on His pure Word in order to defend their faith and sustain their spiritual lives. This book will provide the real spiritual bread of life to all of you who have become the royal people of the King by faith. Through His Church and servants, God will continue to provide you with this bread of life. May God's blessings be on all of you who have been born again of water and the Spirit, who desire to have true

You’ll hear from world-class voices:

Wherever you preach, this is your space to grow as a preacher who speaks with clarity, compassion, and courage.

title: Both Lives Podcast
author: Evangelical Alliance
description:
Welcome to the Both Lives Podcast – where we believe both women and their unborn children matter in every pregnancy.

Through honest and compassionate conversations, we explore fertility, pregnancy, birth, parenting, and the big questions around abortion. Guests from medicine, law, frontline services, and theology bring fresh insight as we look past pro-life vs. pro-choice debates toward a more hopeful, human-centred story.

At the heart of every episode is a belief in the God-given dignity of all life, and a desire to create a better future for women and children before and after birth.

Find out more about Both Lives: bothlives.co.uk


A podcast from the Evangelical Alliance.

title: Layman’s Homily

description: Two brothers, Tony and Brandon D’Arienzo, both devout Catholics and engineers from the Georgia Institute of Technology, invite you into to join their podcast as they address questions stemming from living out our Catholic faith in the modern age. Our mission statement: To leverage the power of mass communication to connect with other Catholics striving for holiness in their daily lives. We started The Layman’s Homily because we are both passionate about our faith, and we both enjoy reading and writing. We hope, by sharing our experiences, that you will be empowered to continue fighting and striving to be a Catholic man or woman, because it is worth fighting for.

Our vision is to empower preachers to engage in faithful and fitting proclamation of the gospel by providing resources, training, and learning opportunities. The Center is housed within Baylor University's George W. Truett Theological Seminary. It was established in 2006 by David and Shirley Lake in memory of their son Kyle.

The ideas and perspectives discussed are then contextualized to South Florida to seek ways local Pentecostal churches can embrace new strategies in social justice to impact the lives of people in society beyond the four walls of the church. Join the Conversation. Write to aumole18@georgefox.edu.

title: Of the Laws of Ecclesiastical Polity, Book 1
author: Richard Hooker
description: In his profound exploration of the English Church, Richard Hooker embarks on a quest to uncover the foundations of Church laws and governance. His seminal work, The Laws of Ecclesiastical Polity, emerges as a formidable response to the Puritan belief that all matters of religious worship, discipline, and governance are dictated solely by Holy Scripture. Hooker challenges this notion, asserting that understanding the Divine order requires not just the written word of God, but also an examination of moral relationships, historical contexts, and societal institutions. He emphasizes the essential role of human reason in interpreting the Bible, distinguishing between what is immutable and what is subject to change, and separating the timeless from the transient. Hookers aim is to resolve the pressing controversies surrounding religion and governance, guiding hearts to follow the light of reasoned judgment, free from prejudice and passion. - Summary by Vernon Staley

title: Joseph Prince Audio Podcast
author: Joseph Prince
description:
With more than two decades of full-time ministry behind him, Joseph Prince is today a leading voice in proclaiming the gospel of grace around the world through his books, teaching resources, and television ministry. 

The author of best sellers such as The Power of Right Believing, Destined To Reign, and Unmerited Favor, Joseph is also a highly sought-after conference speaker. He has impacted church leaders worldwide by preaching the unadulterated gospel of Jesus with boldness. He is known for teaching God's Word in a fresh, practical, and revelatory way that always unveils Jesus. His humorous, dynamic, and engaging style of preaching has also endeared him to a wide spectrum of viewers who tune in to his daily television program. His broadcast currently reaches millions of homes across North America, Europe, Africa, Australia, and Israel on both secular and Christian networks. 

A founding member of New Creation Church, Joseph initially served as an elder and associate pastor. However, his unanimous appointment as senior pastor in 1990 marked a turning point in the history of the church, which started experiencing phenomenal growth. title: The Faith Filled Therapy Podcast
author: The Faith Filled Therapist
description:
Hello to you, I’m glad you’re here. 


The Faith Filled Therapy podcast sits at the intersection of Scripture, neuroscience, and therapeutic insight, exploring how we actually experience change, not just talk about it.


Each episode gently unpacks what it means to renew your mind in a real, lived way,  looking at thought patterns, the nervous system, and the inner world we carry, all through a lens that holds neuroscience with curiosity, while ultimately submitting it to the truth of Scripture.


This isn’t about quick fixes or surface-level faith. It’s about understanding why you feel the way you feel, learning how your mind and body work, and discovering how transformation happens-spirit, soul and synapses.


If you’ve ever loved God but still felt stuck in your thoughts, overwhelmed in your emotions, or unsure how faith connects to your mental health, you’re not alone.


This podcast will help you make sense of your inner world, and take small, meaningful steps towards wholeness.

title: Justin's In Your Corner Bible Teaching
author: Justin Hoff
description:
Here we will dive deep into Biblical study, allow Scripture to interpret Scripture, wherein the Word and Holy Spirit will do the healing, correcting, and prepare hearts for these End Times through Bible prophecy.

In the near future, guests will be invited on the podcast in order to contribute and collaborate. The mission here, God willing, is the creation of this ministry, led by the Lord, His Word, and Holy Spirit, to become a trusted Biblical source for many others to rely on for the Truth. There may be controversial topics where exposing traditions of man and false doctrines from time to time will be in order, which will likely challenge deeply held, yet unbiblical, beliefs. I'll be sure these and other difficult conversations will be conducted with utmost love and respect, for sake of edification as Jesus would want.

From time to time, topics may cover apologetics, social issues, as well as relationships (healthy vs toxic, equally yoked vs not). I'll be sharing personal vlogs; special moments with family and friends. All Glory to God.🙏🏻

Valuable resources to help you in your studies and journey with the Lord:

Bible & Bible

They talk about hearing God’s voice both personally and prophetically, supernatural healing and redemptive suffering, living as empowered witnesses in the ordinary world, and the way of love that binds us together as the Church…among other things. We hope these conversations will be a conduit to deeper experience with the gift of God’s indwelling Spirit.

Join us, as we step in this amazing book together.

This sermon series was preached in 2023.

The Nature of Reality podcast explores the deepest questions about who we are, why we’re here, and how reality works—through the teachings of the Seth Material.

The Seth Material offers one of the deepest, most complete, and dogma-free perspectives on the nature of reality, consciousness, the purpose of physical life, and the creative power of the self.

description: Two New Testament scholars who specialise in Jewish law explore how Jesus interprets and applies the Mosaic law in the canonical gospels.

title: Commentary on the Gospel of Luke, Sermons 57-65 by Cyril of Alexandria
author: Loyal Books
description: Sermons 57 through 65 cover the Gospel According to Luke 9:57 to 10:21. - Summary by the Reader

title: The Sermon On The Mount
author: Pastor Jeremy Higgins
description: This teaching series through the Sermon On The Mount was taught by Pastor Jeremy Higgins at Calvary Community Church in Cedar Rapids, Iowa.  For more information about Pastor Jeremy, or our Church, visit us online at www.CalvaryCedarRapids.com.

title: Preaching In Youth Ministry
author: Preaching in Youth Ministry
description:
Welcome to The Preaching In Youth Ministry Podcast. This is a podcast dedicated to helping youth pastors be faithful preachers. Join us as we cover everything from theology of preaching, developing a sermon calendar, preaching through books of the Bible, and so much more. 

Please send questions, comments, and requests for specific episodes to preachinginyouthministry@gmail.com. 

Thanks for listening. Please subscribe, review, and share with any youth pastor you know!

title: SUNDAY READINGS AND HOMILY
author: ST. ANTHONY CATHOLIC CHURCH NUNGUA
description: Sunday liturgical readings and homilies from St. Anthony Catholic Church, Nungua

title: Gospel Outreach: The Sermons of Tom Peterson
author: Tom Peterson
description: Tom Peterson preached powerful sermons through the 80's and the 2000's from the Bible. Now these awesome messages are being shared with everyone!

title: Expository Preaching: Voices in the Wilderness
author: Antonio Tremayne Dixon, Sr.
description: "Expository Preaching: Voices in the Wilderness" is a podcast focused on equipping preachers, pastors, and those seeking to engage in pastoral ministry with helpful tools and insights regarding expository preaching. While this is the targeted audience, the information shared through these episodes will be beneficial to all who listen. Make sure to subscribe to the available platforms. New episodes will be released every month.

title: Bible Exposition With Lo
author: Bible Exposition With Lo
description:
This is a podcast for every Christian particularly those that are willing to learn and be exposed to the limitless riches of the word.
With the help and guidance of the Holy Spirit, myself and some friends (from time to time) will be explaining scriptures and also address vital issues with respect to our Christian journey.
 Contributions,questions and reviews are welcome. Connect Now!
Email: Oluwayomide@gmail.com

title: Classic Holiness Sermons
author: Dylan Damrell
description: A Podcast of the great Pentecostal Holiness Preachers in the movement. We upload sermons from the great men of God that has blazed the trail for many generations to come. We are attempting to persevere these classic sermons for those that cherish these life changing sermons. Thank you for listening!!

title: Father Hudgins' Homilies
author: Father James Hudgins
description: Homilies given by Father James Hudgins, Pastor of St. Theresa Catholic Church in Ashburn, Virginia. The seven most recent homilies are available via podcast; for the archives, visit www.FatherHudgins.com.

title: The Neville Goddard Podcast
author: Neville Goddard
description: The Neville Goddard Podcast is dedicated to bringing Neville's timeless ideas into a modern, immersive format you can return to again and again. This is a modern faithful interpretation of Neville Goddard's most powerful teachings.

title: Insight with Karl Faase
author: Olive Tree Media
description: Hosted by Karl Faase, Insight delves into the big themes of life, faith, and culture through the power of story. Each conversation opens a window into the experiences of everyday people, leaders, thinkers, and creatives who have navigated life’s challenges and found hope along the way. Rooted in a Christian worldview but open to all, Insight aims to spark thoughtful reflection and encourage deeper engagement with the world around us.

title: Expository Preaching for Impact
author: Tony Guthrie, PHD.
description: Tony Guthrie from Learning and Developing Expository Preaching reveals all of his expository sermon preparation and delivery principles so you can be ahead of the preaching curve and make meaningful biblical impact on your listeners and see them transformed for God's glory. Discover how you can interpret scripture and develop expository messages that cause your congregants to develop a deeper appreciation for God's Word and for living a meaningful and impactful Christian life. Dr. Guthrie will help you develop outlines that work, understand principles of interpretation even better, connect with your listeners with meaningful application, and take your preaching to a higher level. Hit subscribe and get ready to change your life!

title: Bayou Baptist Pulpit Preaching
author: Bayou Drive Baptist Church
description: A friendly church just 30 minutes south of Houston's downtown emphasizing text-driven Bible preaching.

title: The Preaching Lab
author: Clif Johnson
description: Welcome to The Preaching Lab Podcast, where we believe healthy pastors make healthy pulpits. Each episode is designed to encourage pastors toward spiritual health, personal holiness, and faithful exposition of God’s Word. Hosted by Adam Thomas and Jason Goodwin, Baptist pastors and co-leaders of The Preaching Lab Ministry, this podcast offers honest conversations, biblical encouragement, and practical wisdom for those called to preach. Join us as we talk about the soul of the preacher, the craft of expository preaching, and the rhythms that sustain long-term faithfulness in ministry. Whether you’re a seasoned pastor, a student of preaching, or someone longing to grow in biblical exposition, The Preaching Lab Podcast exists to help you stay healthy — in heart, in mind, and in the pulpit.

title: EASTER SERMONS
author: Pastor Robert Griffith
description: Easter Sermons by Pastor Robert Griffith

title: Pentecost Preaching
author: Pentecost Preaching
description: This Podcast is created for Trinitarian Pentecostals from a Holiness Perspective. We post sermons from the Pentecostal Holiness movement. Enjoy our sermons!

title: Preaching Magazine Podcast
author: Preaching Magazine
description: The Preaching Magazine Podcast is where preaching and practice meet. Each episode features conversations with pastors, scholars, and ministry leaders who share insights on the craft of preaching, the challenges facing today’s church, and the timeless power of God’s Word. Whether you’re stepping into the pulpit for the first time or have decades of ministry experience, this podcast will equip, encourage, and inspire you to preach with clarity, conviction, and creativity.

title: Doctrine at Work
author: Reformed Theological Seminary
description: Doctrine at Work is the companion podcast for the Institute for Faith Work and Culture's annual publication entitled Studies in Faith, Work, and Culture. The journal reflects original essays presented during The Colloquium, an annual gathering hosted by the IFWC that brings together practitioners from the Faith & Work community, RTS seminary students, academic scholars, and marketplace leaders.

title: Christ Covenant PC Sunday Sermons
author: Christ Covenant Presbyterian Church
description: Christ Covenant Presbyterian Church of the Greater Atlanta area currently worships and serves in Peachtree Corners, GA. We are part of the Presbyterian Church in America.

title: Preach!
author: Cristiano Goncalves

title: Clear Preaching
author: Dr Jonathan McClintock
description:
You worked hard on that sermon. Did they actually hear it?


Clear Preaching is the podcast for preachers who are serious about closing the gap between what they meant to say and what their congregation actually heard. Hosted by Dr. Jonathan McClintock — preacher, pastor, sixteen-year homiletics instructor, and developer of the four-domain Clear Preaching Framework — each episode delivers practical, framework-driven teaching on the discipline of preaching with clarity.


Through solo teaching episodes, conversations with preachers and scholars, and real sermon analysis, Clear Preaching helps you develop clarity at every stage of the preaching process — from the moment you open the text in your study to the moment you close your Bible in the pulpit.


Whether you are stepping into the pulpit for the first time or have been preaching for decades — if you believe the message you carry is worth delivering as clearly as possible, this podcast is for you.

title: THE CHRIST - An Easter Audio Epic
author: Faith Podcast Network
description: From the power halls of Rome to the back roads of Galilee, THE CHRIST traces the life, death, and legacy of Jesus of Nazareth. Through betrayal, courage, suffering, and hope, the series explores how one man's story reshaped history—and redefined love, authority, and sacrifice.

title: Fire Church MSE Podcast
author: Fire Church Melbourne South East
description: Fire Church Melbourne South East is a powerful and vibrant church in the South East of Melbourne — Be encouraged weekly with our Sunday service messages full of faith, truth, and practical encouragement.

title: The Foundations of the Catholic Faith – ST PAUL REPOSITORY
author: Fr Guy de Gaynesford STL
description: DEDICATED TO TEACHING THE CATHOLIC FAITH

title: GCC Sermon Podcast
author: Gospel Community Church
description: Podcast by Gospel Community Church

title: Jesus Is Lord (live Preaching)
author: Jesus Is Lord Church
description: Faith Comes by hearing, and hearing by the word of God. Romans 10:17

title: Telugu Christian Sermons
author: John Vunnamatla
description: A Christian Sermon Podcast for Telugu speaking people by Pastor John Vunnamatla. Pas. John is the pastor of Indian Baptist Church. He is the son of a renowned preacher in the 90's, Rev. Dr. V. Daivabhimani. Pastor John graduated with a Bachelors and a Masters degree from a renowned institution in Phoenix, AZ. He now serves with his wife Emily in Andhra Pradesh, India. They have a beautiful daughter Alyssa.

title: Just Homilies
author: Just Homilies
description: Mini sermons for the disillusioned, the deconstructed, the tired-of-church, or those who’ve never stepped inside one, but still feel drawn to explore Christian spirituality through God’s Word. No matter how you arrive, this space is for you.

title: Pastor Kenny's Sermons
author: Ken Campbell
description: Podcast by Ken Campbell

title: Parkside Gospel Church Sermons
author: Parkside Gospel Church
description: Podcast by Parkside Gospel Church

title: Sermons Out of Rural Ministry
author: Pastor Josh Ehrler
description: Dynamic, contextual sermons from Pastor Josh Ehrler, who serves a small town ELCA Lutheran congregation in Northern Illinois. Sermons are rooted in scripture and reflect the local community, as well as the movements of the wider world. He and his congregation are affirming of LGBTQ+ personhood and live out their baptismal promise to seek (racial, economic, identity, environmental) justice and peace.

title: HOELY HOMILY
author: Rae Izah
description: Welcome to HOELY HOMILY , a podcast where we freely discuss about everything life , fun, facts and of course cruise.

title: EG Sunday Sermon
author: Sheldon Williams
description: Podcast by Sheldon Williams

title: Blessed & Stressed
author: Sheyi Martins-Allen & Dr. Katie Gaddini
description:
Blessed & Stressed: Religion & Politics Across the Pond is a transatlantic podcast exploring faith and politics in the United States and the United Kingdom.


Hosted by Dr. Katie Gaddini, sociologist and author of Esther’s Army: The Christian Women Who Power the American Right, and Sheyi Martins-Allen of the viral Friday Rants, the show dives into religion, power, identity, and the forces shaping public life on both sides of the Atlantic.

title: Words for Life Christian Sermons
author: Songs of Hope
description: Christian talks, sermons and teaching from a weekly radio program in Melbourne, Australia called "Songs of Hope". The talks are sourced from local pastors in Melbourne. Talk time varies from 5 minutes to 25 minutes.
